title: Low-order formal syntax checks
summary: Implements a limited syntax normalizer and machine-checks that the recursively generated formal expressions at orders one and two match explicitly written raw P₂ and P₄ syntax trees.

mathematicalContent.heading: Limited normalizer
mathematicalContent.body: The normalizer removes zeros and ones and multiplies rational literals. It intentionally does not reassociate, commute, or distribute products and sums.
mathematicalContent.formula: 0+E\mapsto E,\quad1\cdot E\mapsto E,\quad q\cdot r\mapsto qr.

mathematicalContent.heading: Order two
mathematicalContent.body: For the ℓ=2 recursion, the first solved jet is recorded in raw syntax.

mathematicalContent.heading: Checked outputs
mathematicalContent.body: Lean uses native_decide to compare normalized syntax trees for formalP 1 and formalP 2 with the explicit raw expressions.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Normalizes a rational literal to syntactic zero, one, or a nontrivial rational node.
Defines the restricted recursive syntax normalizer used for low-order decidable comparisons.
Records the expected first solved jet in the ℓ=2 calculation.
Records the raw expected formal P₂ before distributing the factor −1.
Records an expanded display syntax for the expected P₂ expression.
Records the raw expected P₄ syntax with D₀ left unapplied to products.
Machine-checks equality of the normalized order-one syntax trees.
Machine-checks equality of the normalized order-two syntax trees.

references.locator: Formal Juhl-type formula and OR bridge
references.note: Primary source for the intended curved OR–Juhl identity.
Source for the Juhl side of the formal syntax.

limitations: The checks concern raw FormalExpr syntax under a deliberately weak normalizer. They do not prove the usual geometric P₂ or P₄ formulas, expand D₀ by a Leibniz rule, compare curvature conventions, or establish the general Juhl identity.
